language.label: Language
language.hint: Applies to this browser. Text updates immediately.

login.title: My Studio Desk
login.subtitle: Sign in once—your desk follows you on every device.
login.resetSuccess: Check your email. If {{email}} is registered, Firebase sent a link to set a new password. Look in Spam too. Then return here and sign in.
login.resetHint: Enter the email you use for this desk, then send a reset link.
login.errPasswordShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
login.errEmailInUse: That email is already registered. Sign in instead.
login.errUserNotFound: No account found for that email. Try New account or check spelling.
login.errTooManyRequests: Too many attempts. Wait a few minutes and try again.

settings.title: Settings
settings.studioNameHint: Shown in the header and login screen on this device. Leave empty to use the default name.
settings.intro: Desk alerts run in this tab when the browser allows notifications. They fire for tomorrow's jobs or visits and for past-event payment due—not on a fixed schedule every minute.
settings.deskAlertsHint: Store preference on this device. You still need browser permission below.
settings.deniedHint: Notifications blocked by browser. Allow this site from browser settings (lock icon or site settings), then reload.
settings.testBody: If you see this, browser alerts are working. Real alerts only when you have tomorrow jobs / visits or payment-due orders.

backup.syncHelpBeforeRulesFile: Open Firebase → Firestore → Rules and publish the rules from
backup.syncHelpAfterRulesFile: in this project. If this device uses a hosted website, add the same
backup.syncHelpAfterEnv: keys in the host (Vercel/Netlify env) and rebuild—otherwise this device stays offline-only.
backup.localOnly: Data is stored in this browser only (localStorage)—not shared across devices. Add .env.local + login for cloud sync.

clients.title: Clients
clients.lead: People you shoot for—used on every order and payment line.
clients.emptyText: Tap here or fill the form below—takes a few seconds.
clients.rosterLead: {{count}} on file — used when you book orders and log payments.
clients.rosterPlaceholder: Your roster will show up here.
clients.deleteConfirm: Delete {{name}}? All orders linked to this client will be removed.

orders.title: Orders
orders.lead: Quote, booking date, optional event from → to for multi-day functions (e.g. wedding), venue/address, and client payments. Status updates from dates and balance (Booked → In progress → Payment pending → Closed).
orders.almostText: Add a client first—then you can attach orders and track payments.
orders.noOrdersText: Tap to jump to the form and log your first booking.
orders.needClient: Add a client to create orders.
orders.detailEmpty: Pick a job on the left to edit the quote and client payments.
orders.statusHint: — from event dates and amount due.
orders.eventToHint: For single-day events, leave "Event to" empty. For weddings across several days, set both.
orders.exposureTitle: Who is coming to your studio for this Order
orders.exposureIntro: Add everyone who will be at your place for this job. When you go to someone else's location, use the My Exposing tab instead. If you owe them money for the exposure, enter it under Pay them. Use the same Match key on My Exposing (or the same spelling) so the dashboard can net what you collect minus what you pay.
orders.matchKeyPlaceholder: e.g. sandip — same on My Exposing

field.title: My Exposing
field.lead: Outside shoot: who, where, how much they pay you. Multi-day? Set To date.
field.tipBody: If you also Pay them on an order (exposure guest), use the same match key on both. The card then shows what you collect, what you owe on the order, and the net.
field.matchPlaceholder: Same as order Pay them row, e.g. sandip
field.emptyText: Nothing saved yet. Add one above when you book an outside job.
field.emptyJumpHint: Tap here to jump to the form.

dash.title: Dashboard
dash.lead: Dues, outside visits, and quick adds—at a glance.
dash.thisWeekLead: Calendar week (Mon–Sun): jobs and outside visits that touch these dates.
dash.welcomeCopy: Add a client and an order for studio jobs—or open My Exposing for outside shoots (whose place, what to collect).
dash.upcomingOrdersSub: Jobs whose event is still running or coming up (end date today or later—or no event date yet). After the event, once fully paid, they become Closed and leave this list. Past events with a balance due stay in Orders until paid (status updates there).
dash.noUpcomingText: Nothing on the calendar through today for open jobs. Orders with payment still due after the event still appear in Orders with amount due until settled.
dash.noEventDate: No event date — set in order
dash.visitCollectionsSub: Upcoming shoots where you still need to collect.
dash.noVisitsText: Log My Exposing with an amount to see it here.
dash.balancesSub: Studio = all client orders with something left to collect. My Exposing = gross still due on each outside visit (amount − collected), all dates. Combined is a simple sum — not the "same person" net from the Pending (visits) tile.
dash.noStudioBalances: No studio balances by client right now. Use My Exposing for outside collections.
dash.settleSub: Field due minus studio Pay them when it's the same contact (name or Match key).
dash.settleHint: Studio pay and visit money both exist, but nothing linked—use the same Match key on the guest and the visit (or similar names).
dash.settleEmptyText: Add "Pay them" on an exposure guest and My Exposing for the same person.
dash.orderWhenTitle: Event / shoot dates for this order
dash.tileCollectTitle: Studio order dues plus My Exposing gross (every row). Not the same-person visit net tile.
dash.guestPayTitle: Same-person My Exposing due reduces Pay them in this total.
